perturbation.original_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, unless Vendor independently develops the same information; and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all copies remain Ingram's property. The change introduces an exception: if the Vendor independently develops the same information, it is no longer subject to Ingram's ownership, directly contradicting the beginning of the sentence and creating ambiguity regarding ownership of replicated information. This means that at the begining of the sentence 'Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram' now is contradicted with 'unless Vendor independently develops the same information'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, unless Vendor independently develops the same information; and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that all copies of proprietary information belong to Ingram. The changed text introduces an exception: if the Vendor independently develops the same information, it is no longer subject to Ingram's ownership. This directly contradicts the beginning of the sentence and creates ambiguity regarding ownership of replicated information, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'. Since the changed text and contradicted text are in the same location the contradiction is clear.

perturbation.original_text: Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party, including, but not limited to Vendor's parent, subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party, except Vendor's parent company. Vendor may disclose Proprietary Information to Vendor's parent company, and Vendor’s parent company can disclose such proprietary information to any third party including, but not limited to Vendor's subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.
perturbation.explanation: Initially, all third parties are restricted from receiving Proprietary Information. The modified clause permits disclosure to the Vendor's parent company and further allows Vendor’s parent company disclose Proprietary Information to any third party, including subsidiaries and affiliated companies. This creates an internal contradiction within the same sentence, as it simultaneously prohibits and permits disclosure to certain entities, leading to ambiguity. The initial part of the sentence 'Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party' contradicts with 'Vendor may disclose Proprietary Information to Vendor's parent company, and Vendor’s parent company can disclose such proprietary information to any third party including, but not limited to Vendor's subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party, except Vendor's parent company. Vendor may disclose Proprietary Information to Vendor's parent company, and Vendor’s parent company can disclose such proprietary information to any third party including, but not limited to Vendor's subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself by first prohibiting the disclosure of Proprietary Information to any third party and then immediately allowing the disclosure to Vendor's parent company, which can then disclose to any third party (including subsidiaries or affiliated companies). This is a clear and direct conflict within the same clause, fitting the definition of a 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text is the exact changed text, where it contradicts itself. The original text prohibited disclosure to all third parties.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information, unless Ingram provides express written consent allowing Vendor to do so, under conditions including but not limited to revenue-sharing agreements. Vendor is prohibited from contacting the customer even if express written consent is given by Ingram.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause prohibits direct contact for soliciting or selling. The modified text introduces an exception where Ingram can provide written consent, seemingly allowing contact. However, it then negates this exception by stating that Vendor is still prohibited from contacting the customer, even with consent. The contradiction arises from introducing an exception and immediately retracting it, creating confusion about whether contact is ever permitted. Specifically, it is contradicted because 'Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information' is in contradiction with 'unless Ingram provides express written consent allowing Vendor to do so', but then the prior part is restored with 'Vendor is prohibited from contacting the customer even if express written consent is given by Ingram.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor agrees not to use, or permit others to use, the Proprietary Information, other than for the purpose of monitoring sales through distribution. Vendor agrees to make no more than five (5) copies of the Proprietary Information unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties; and Vendor may distribute and provide access to the Proprietary Information to all of Vendor's personnel, regardless of whether they require access to Proprietary Information for the foregoing purpose. Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information, unless Ingram provides express written consent allowing Vendor to do so, under conditions including but not limited to revenue-sharing agreements. Vendor is prohibited from contacting the customer even if express written consent is given by Ingram.
perturbation.justification: The changed text initially introduces an exception to the prohibition of direct contact with customers, stating that Ingram can provide written consent. However, it immediately retracts this exception by adding that the vendor is still prohibited from contacting the customer, even with Ingram's consent. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section, as the clause both allows and disallows contact under the same circumstances. This change directly breaks compatibility with the 'Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information' by first creating 'unless Ingram provides express written consent allowing Vendor to do so' and immediately negating it 'Vendor is prohibited from contacting the customer even if express written consent is given by Ingram.'

perturbation.original_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of the whole Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of the whole Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California, except that any disputes related to intellectual property rights will be governed by the laws of Delaware. However, the laws of California will still apply to intellectual property disputes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text designates California law for all aspects of the agreement. The modified text introduces an exception for intellectual property disputes, assigning them to Delaware law. It then immediately contradicts this exception by stating that California law still applies to intellectual property disputes. The introduction of an exception and immediate retraction causes confusion. The text 'The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of the whole Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California' is contradicted by 'except that any disputes related to intellectual property rights will be governed by the laws of Delaware', and then the prior section is re-established when introducing 'However, the laws of California will still apply to intellectual property disputes.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of the whole Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California, except that any disputes related to intellectual property rights will be governed by the laws of Delaware. However, the laws of California will still apply to intellectual property disputes. In the event that any provision is found invalid or unenforceable pursuant to Domestic Distribution Agreement March 15, 1996 Form 403 Ingram Micro (3/15/96) 108 <PAGE> 13 statutory or Judicial decree, such provision shall be construed only to the maximum extent permitted by law, and the remainder of the Agreement shall be valid and enforceable in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text specifies that California law governs the entire agreement. The changed text initially introduces an exception, stating that Delaware law governs intellectual property disputes, which contradicts the original blanket statement. Then, it immediately retracts the exception by stating California law still applies to intellectual property disputes, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This fluctuation creates a clear and impactful contradiction that would cause confusion, unenforceability, or a legal issue within the document, thus meeting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The contradicted text is in the same place as the changed text, which emphasizes how the modified section now directly contradicts itself, and therefore contradicts the original section.
